title: New data and statistics legislation | Stats NZ
content:
## New data and statistics legislation
The Data and Statistics Act 2022 supports a well-functioning data and statistics system that makes the best use of data collected and held by government while ensuring private and confidential information is held securely and used appropriately.

## Data and Statistics Bill now an Act
The Data and Statistics Act 2022 ca me into force on 1 September 2022 and replaced the Stat is tics Act 1975. The Act was informed by a review of statistics legislation and public consultation on high-level proposals for new data and statistics legislation.
The Data and Statistics Act:
- recognises the Crown's responsibility to consider and provide for Māori interests in data and statistics
- enables more effective data system leadership
- strengthens and future-proofs the framework for collecting data for official statistics
- modernises the framework for accessing data for research
- continues to provide appropriate safeguards and protections to ensure public trust and confidence in the collection and use of data for official statistics and research.

## Developing the Data and Statistics Act 2022
The following steps informed the drafting and finalisation of the new Act and its associated policies.

### Public feedback on proposed changes to legislation
In 2018, the Minister of Statistics and the Government Statistician invited public consultation on high-level proposals presented in Towards new data and statistics legislation: Public discussion document. This feedback informed development of the Data and Statistics Bill.
Read Towards new data and statistics legislation: Summary of submissions on 2018 consultation (published in April 2019).

### Broad policy proposals
In March 2020, Cabinet agreed to broad policy proposals for new data and statistics legislation and to the drafting of a new Data and Statistics Bill.
Read Cabinet papers – new data and statistics legislation policy proposals

### Supplementary policy proposals
In April 2021, Cabinet agreed supplementary policy proposals including offences, penalties, and amendments to other legislation to enable provision of data to Stats NZ.
Read Cabinet paper – Data and Statistics Legislation: Supplementary policy proposals

### Introduction of the Bill to House of Representatives
In September 2021, Cabinet approved the introduction of the Data and Statistics Bill to the House of Representatives.
Read Cabinet papers – Data and Statistics Bill

### Data and Statistics Bill
The Bill had its First Reading on 9 November 2021 and was referred to the Governance and Administration Select Committee. The Committee considered submissions and reported back on 9 May 2022. The B ill passed its Second Reading on 17 May 2022 and had Committee of the w hole House on 27 July 2022. The Bill passed the final debate and vot e at the Third Reading on 02 August 2022. The Bill received Royal Assent on 08 August to become the Data and Statistics Act 2022.
Submissions and advice through the Parliamentary process from when the Data and Statistics Bill was introduced in the House to Royal Assent.
Read Data and Statistics Bill - Progress of the Bill.
